scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: The skill appears largely benign and focused on standard release automation guidance. No direct prompt injection, credential theft, exfiltration, command injection implementation, obfuscation, or malicious tool chaining was identified. The main issues are low-severity packaging and metadata gaps: missing optional manifest fields and inconsistent referenced-file listings. Operationally, the skill does include potentially impactful release commands (`git push`, `gh release create`), but these are aligned with the stated purpose rather than covert or malicious behavior.

meta_correlations.relationship: All findings concern metadata/package clarity rather than exploitable behavior: missing referenced-file consistency, absent tool/compatibility declarations, and missing license.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Update the skill package metadata and documentation: add a proper manifest block with license, compatibility, and allowed-tools; remove or correct any stale referenced-file entries so the package contents match the instructions.

meta_recommendations.title: Add explicit manifest metadata
meta_recommendations.fix:
Add a YAML frontmatter manifest to SKILL.md such as: `---
name: release-manager
description: Manage software releases with semantic versioning and changelog generation
license: MIT
compatibility: [Claude.ai, Claude Code]
allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Write]
---`.

meta_recommendations.title: Remove or correct stale referenced-file entries
meta_recommendations.fix: Ensure the package inventory only lists files that actually exist. If `git.py`, `a.py`, and `the.py` are not part of the skill, delete those references from packaging metadata or analysis input; if they are required, include them in the skill directory and document their purpose in SKILL.md.

meta_risk_assessment.summary: The skill contains standard release-management instructions and no clear malicious behavior; the reported issues are documentation/manifest hygiene gaps rather than security threats.
meta_risk_assessment.top_priority: Add explicit manifest metadata for allowed tools, compatibility, and license to improve transparency and reviewability.
meta_risk_assessment.verdict_reasoning: SKILL.md only documents ordinary git/npm/gh release steps, includes safety-oriented guidance, and shows no prompt injection, exfiltration, obfuscation, or unauthorized code execution. The flagged items are non-security informational issues.
